seoTitle: How to Draw in Google Docs (2026)
metaDescription: Learn how to create shapes, illustrations, and diagrams inside Google Docs using the built-in Drawing tool without external design software.
intro: You don't need to leave Google Docs or use outside design software to add custom shapes and diagrams to a document. This guide covers using the built-in Drawing canvas to create and style vector graphics directly inline. It's for anyone building callout boxes, custom diagrams, or simple illustrations.

steps.heading: Open the Drawing tool
steps.body: Click Insert, hover over Drawing, and select New to open the canvas.

steps.heading: Choose a shape
steps.body: Click the Shapes selector icon to open the design sub-menu and pick a standard geometric shape.

steps.heading: Draw and style your shape
steps.body: Click and drag to draw the shape, then use the neighboring fill color and border weight tools to customize its appearance.

steps.heading: Add text labels
steps.body: Use the Text Box tool to inject labels and typography directly inside your shapes.

steps.heading: Group elements and save
steps.body: Select multiple elements, right-click, and choose Group to lock them together, then click Save and Close to embed the drawing.

faq.question: Can I edit a drawing after inserting it into my document?
faq.answer: Yes, double-clicking the embedded drawing reopens the canvas so you can make further edits anytime.

faq.question: How do I keep multiple shapes together when moving them?
faq.answer: Select the shapes, right-click, and choose Group so they move and resize together as one unit.

faq.question: Can I change how text wraps around my drawing?
faq.answer: Yes, you can adjust text-wrapping layout options to align your illustration seamlessly within the page.

faq.question: Does the Drawing tool support custom colors?
faq.answer: Yes, you can customize fill opacity, border weights, and hex color values for each shape you add.
